texts.orig: Providers and prospective providers participating in the AI regulatory sandbox shall remain liable under applicable Union and national liability law for any damage inflicted on third parties as a result of the experimentation taking place in the sandbox. However, provided that the prospective providers observe the specific plan and the terms and conditions for their participation and follow in good faith the guidance given by the national competent authority, no administrative fines shall be imposed by the authorities for infringements of this Regulation. Where other competent authorities responsible for other Union and national law were actively involved in the supervision of the AI system in the sandbox and provided guidance for compliance, no administrative fines shall be imposed regarding that law.
texts.text: Providers and prospective providers participating in the AI regulatory sandbox shall remain liable under applicable Union and national liability law for any damage inflicted on third parties as a result of the experimentation taking place in the sandbox. However, provided that the prospective providers observe the specific plan and the terms and conditions for their participation and follow in good faith the guidance given by the national competent authority, no administrative fines shall be imposed by the authorities for infringements of this Regulation. Where other competent authorities responsible for other Union and national law were actively involved in the supervision of the AI system in the sandbox and provided guidance for compliance, no administrative fines shall be imposed regarding that law.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The AI regulatory sandboxes shall be designed and implemented in such a way that, where relevant, they facilitate cross-border cooperation between national competent authorities.
texts.text: The AI regulatory sandboxes shall be designed and implemented in such a way that, where relevant, they facilitate cross-border cooperation between national competent authorities.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: National competent authorities shall coordinate their activities and cooperate within the framework of the Board.
texts.text: National competent authorities shall coordinate their activities and cooperate within the framework of the Board.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: National competent authorities shall inform the AI Office and the Board of the establishment of a sandbox, and may ask them for support and guidance. The AI Office shall make publicly available a list of planned and existing sandboxes and keep it up to date in order to encourage more interaction in the AI regulatory sandboxes and cross-border cooperation.
texts.text: National competent authorities shall inform the AI Office and the Board of the establishment of a sandbox, and may ask them for support and guidance. The AI Office shall make publicly available a list of planned and existing sandboxes and keep it up to date in order to encourage more interaction in the AI regulatory sandboxes and cross-border cooperation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: National competent authorities shall submit annual reports to the AI Office and to the Board, from one year after the establishment of the AI regulatory sandbox and every year thereafter until its termination, and a final report. Those reports shall provide information on the progress and results of the implementation of those sandboxes, including best practices, incidents, lessons learnt and recommendations on their setup and, where relevant, on the application and possible revision of this Regulation, including its delegated and implementing acts, and on the application of other Union law supervised by the competent authorities within the sandbox. The national competent authorities shall make those annual reports or abstracts thereof available to the public, online. The Commission shall, where appropriate, take the annual reports into account when exercising its tasks under this Regulation.
texts.text: National competent authorities shall submit annual reports to the AI Office and to the Board, from one year after the establishment of the AI regulatory sandbox and every year thereafter until its termination, and a final report. Those reports shall provide information on the progress and results of the implementation of those sandboxes, including best practices, incidents, lessons learnt and recommendations on their setup and, where relevant, on the application and possible revision of this Regulation, including its delegated and implementing acts, and on the application of other Union law supervised by the competent authorities within the sandbox. The national competent authorities shall make those annual reports or abstracts thereof available to the public, online. The Commission shall, where appropriate, take the annual reports into account when exercising its tasks under this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall develop a single and dedicated interface containing all relevant information related to AI regulatory sandboxes to allow stakeholders to interact with AI regulatory sandboxes and to raise enquiries with competent authorities, and to seek non-binding guidance on the conformity of innovative products, services, business models embedding AI technologies, in accordance with Article 62(1), point (c). The Commission shall proactively coordinate with national competent authorities, where relevant.
texts.text: The Commission shall develop a single and dedicated interface containing all relevant information related to AI regulatory sandboxes to allow stakeholders to interact with AI regulatory sandboxes and to raise enquiries with competent authorities, and to seek non-binding guidance on the conformity of innovative products, services, business models embedding AI technologies, in accordance with Article 62(1), point (c). The Commission shall proactively coordinate with national competent authorities, where relevant.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Detailed arrangements for, and functioning of, AI regulatory sandboxes
texts.text: Detailed arrangements for, and functioning of, AI regulatory sandboxes

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In order to avoid fragmentation across the Union, the Commission shall adopt implementing acts specifying the detailed arrangements for the establishment, development, implementation, operation and supervision of the AI regulatory sandboxes. The implementing acts shall include common principles on the following issues:
texts.text: In order to avoid fragmentation across the Union, the Commission shall adopt implementing acts specifying the detailed arrangements for the establishment, development, implementation, operation and supervision of the AI regulatory sandboxes. The implementing acts shall include common principles on the following issues:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) eligibility and selection criteria for participation in the AI regulatory sandbox;
texts.text: (a) eligibility and selection criteria for participation in the AI regulatory sandbox;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) procedures for the application, participation, monitoring, exiting from and termination of the AI regulatory sandbox, including the sandbox plan and the exit report;
texts.text: (b) procedures for the application, participation, monitoring, exiting from and termination of the AI regulatory sandbox, including the sandbox plan and the exit report;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the terms and conditions applicable to the participants.
texts.text: (c) the terms and conditions applicable to the participants.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Those implementing acts shall  be  adopted  in  accordance  with  the  examination procedure  referred  to in  Article  98(2).
texts.text: Those implementing acts shall  be  adopted  in  accordance  with  the  examination procedure  referred  to in  Article  98(2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The implementing acts referred to in paragraph 1 shall ensure:
texts.text: The implementing acts referred to in paragraph 1 shall ensure:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) that AI regulatory sandboxes are open to any applying provider or prospective provider of an AI system who fulfils eligibility and selection criteria, which shall be transparent and fair, and that national competent authorities inform applicants of their decision within three months of the application;
texts.text: (a) that AI regulatory sandboxes are open to any applying provider or prospective provider of an AI system who fulfils eligibility and selection criteria, which shall be transparent and fair, and that national competent authorities inform applicants of their decision within three months of the application;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) that AI regulatory sandboxes allow broad and equal access and keep up with demand for participation; providers and prospective providers may also submit applications in partnerships with deployers and other relevant third parties;
texts.text: (b) that AI regulatory sandboxes allow broad and equal access and keep up with demand for participation; providers and prospective providers may also submit applications in partnerships with deployers and other relevant third parties;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) that the detailed arrangements for, and conditions concerning AI regulatory sandboxes support, to the best extent possible, flexibility for national competent authorities to establish and operate their AI regulatory sandboxes;
texts.text: (c) that the detailed arrangements for, and conditions concerning AI regulatory sandboxes support, to the best extent possible, flexibility for national competent authorities to establish and operate their AI regulatory sandboxes;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) that access to the AI regulatory sandboxes is free of charge for SMEs, including start-ups, without prejudice to exceptional costs that national competent authorities may recover in a fair and proportionate manner;
texts.text: (d) that access to the AI regulatory sandboxes is free of charge for SMEs, including start-ups, without prejudice to exceptional costs that national competent authorities may recover in a fair and proportionate manner;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) that they facilitate providers and prospective providers, by means of the learning outcomes of the AI regulatory sandboxes, in complying with conformity assessment obligations under this Regulation and the voluntary application of the codes of conduct referred to in Article 95;
texts.text: (e) that they facilitate providers and prospective providers, by means of the learning outcomes of the AI regulatory sandboxes, in complying with conformity assessment obligations under this Regulation and the voluntary application of the codes of conduct referred to in Article 95;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) that AI regulatory sandboxes facilitate the involvement of other relevant actors within the AI ecosystem, such as notified bodies and standardisation organisations, SMEs, including start-ups, enterprises, innovators, testing and experimentation facilities, research and experimentation labs and European Digital Innovation Hubs, centres of excellence, individual researchers, in order to allow and facilitate cooperation with the public and private sectors;
texts.text: (f) that AI regulatory sandboxes facilitate the involvement of other relevant actors within the AI ecosystem, such as notified bodies and standardisation organisations, SMEs, including start-ups, enterprises, innovators, testing and experimentation facilities, research and experimentation labs and European Digital Innovation Hubs, centres of excellence, individual researchers, in order to allow and facilitate cooperation with the public and private sectors;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) that procedures, processes and administrative requirements for application, selection, participation and exiting the AI regulatory sandbox are simple, easily intelligible, and clearly communicated in order to facilitate the participation of SMEs, including start-ups, with limited legal and administrative capacities and are streamlined across the Union, in order to avoid fragmentation and that participation in an AI regulatory sandbox established by a Member State, or by the European Data Protection Supervisor is mutually and uniformly recognised and carries the same legal effects across the Union;
texts.text: (g) that procedures, processes and administrative requirements for application, selection, participation and exiting the AI regulatory sandbox are simple, easily intelligible, and clearly communicated in order to facilitate the participation of SMEs, including start-ups, with limited legal and administrative capacities and are streamlined across the Union, in order to avoid fragmentation and that participation in an AI regulatory sandbox established by a Member State, or by the European Data Protection Supervisor is mutually and uniformly recognised and carries the same legal effects across the Union;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) that participation in the AI regulatory sandbox is limited to a period that is appropriate to the complexity and scale of the project and that may be extended by the national competent authority;
texts.text: (h) that participation in the AI regulatory sandbox is limited to a period that is appropriate to the complexity and scale of the project and that may be extended by the national competent authority;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) that AI regulatory sandboxes facilitate the development of tools and infrastructure for testing, benchmarking, assessing and explaining dimensions of AI systems relevant for regulatory learning, such as accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity, as well as measures to mitigate risks to fundamental rights and society at large.
texts.text: (i) that AI regulatory sandboxes facilitate the development of tools and infrastructure for testing, benchmarking, assessing and explaining dimensions of AI systems relevant for regulatory learning, such as accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity, as well as measures to mitigate risks to fundamental rights and society at large.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Prospective providers in the AI regulatory sandboxes, in particular SMEs and start-ups, shall be directed, where relevant, to pre-deployment services such as guidance on the implementation of this Regulation, to other value-adding services such as help with standardisation documents and certification, testing and experimentation facilities, European Digital Innovation Hubs and centres of excellence.
texts.text: Prospective providers in the AI regulatory sandboxes, in particular SMEs and start-ups, shall be directed, where relevant, to pre-deployment services such as guidance on the implementation of this Regulation, to other value-adding services such as help with standardisation documents and certification, testing and experimentation facilities, European Digital Innovation Hubs and centres of excellence.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where national competent authorities consider authorising testing in real world conditions supervised within the framework of an AI regulatory sandbox to be established under this Article, they shall specifically agree the terms and conditions of such testing and, in particular, the appropriate safeguards with the participants, with a view to protecting fundamental rights, health and safety. Where appropriate, they shall cooperate with other national competent authorities with a view to ensuring consistent practices across the Union.
texts.text: Where national competent authorities consider authorising testing in real world conditions supervised within the framework of an AI regulatory sandbox to be established under this Article, they shall specifically agree the terms and conditions of such testing and, in particular, the appropriate safeguards with the participants, with a view to protecting fundamental rights, health and safety. Where appropriate, they shall cooperate with other national competent authorities with a view to ensuring consistent practices across the Union.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  59
texts.text: Article  59

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Further processing of personal data for developing certain AI systems in the public interest in the AI regulatory sandbox
texts.text: Further processing of personal data for developing certain AI systems in the public interest in the AI regulatory sandbox

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In the AI regulatory sandbox, personal data lawfully collected for other purposes may be processed solely for the purpose of developing, training and testing certain AI systems in the sandbox when all of the following conditions are met:
texts.text: In the AI regulatory sandbox, personal data lawfully collected for other purposes may be processed solely for the purpose of developing, training and testing certain AI systems in the sandbox when all of the following conditions are met:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) AI systems shall be developed for safeguarding substantial public interest by a public authority or another natural or legal person and in one or more of the following areas:
texts.text: (a) AI systems shall be developed for safeguarding substantial public interest by a public authority or another natural or legal person and in one or more of the following areas:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) public safety and public health, including disease detection, diagnosis prevention, control and treatment and improvement of health care systems;
texts.text: (i) public safety and public health, including disease detection, diagnosis prevention, control and treatment and improvement of health care systems;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (ii) a high level of protection and improvement of the quality of the environment, protection of biodiversity, protection against pollution, green transition measures, climate change mitigation and adaptation measures;
texts.text: (ii) a high level of protection and improvement of the quality of the environment, protection of biodiversity, protection against pollution, green transition measures, climate change mitigation and adaptation measures;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (iii) energy sustainability;
texts.text: (iii) energy sustainability;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (iv) safety and resilience of transport systems and mobility, critical infrastructure and networks;
texts.text: (iv) safety and resilience of transport systems and mobility, critical infrastructure and networks;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (v) efficiency and quality of public administration and public services;
texts.text: (v) efficiency and quality of public administration and public services;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the data processed are necessary for complying with one or more of the requirements referred to in Chapter III, Section 2 where those requirements cannot effectively be fulfilled by processing anonymised, synthetic or other non-personal data;
texts.text: (b) the data processed are necessary for complying with one or more of the requirements referred to in Chapter III, Section 2 where those requirements cannot effectively be fulfilled by processing anonymised, synthetic or other non-personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) there are effective monitoring mechanisms to identify if any high risks to the rights and freedoms of the data subjects, as referred to in Article 35 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 and in Article 39 of Regulation (EU) 2018/1725, may arise during the sandbox experimentation, as well as response mechanisms to promptly mitigate those risks and, where necessary, stop the processing;
texts.text: (c) there are effective monitoring mechanisms to identify if any high risks to the rights and freedoms of the data subjects, as referred to in Article 35 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 and in Article 39 of Regulation (EU) 2018/1725, may arise during the sandbox experimentation, as well as response mechanisms to promptly mitigate those risks and, where necessary, stop the processing;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) any personal data to be processed in the context of the sandbox are in a functionally separate, isolated and protected data processing environment under the control of the prospective provider and only authorised persons have access to those data;
texts.text: (d) any personal data to be processed in the context of the sandbox are in a functionally separate, isolated and protected data processing environment under the control of the prospective provider and only authorised persons have access to those data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) providers can further share the originally collected data only in accordance with Union data protection law; any personal data created in the sandbox cannot be shared outside the sandbox;
texts.text: (e) providers can further share the originally collected data only in accordance with Union data protection law; any personal data created in the sandbox cannot be shared outside the sandbox;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) any processing of personal data in the context of the sandbox neither leads to measures or decisions affecting the data subjects nor does it affect the application of their rights laid down in Union law on the protection of personal data;
texts.text: (f) any processing of personal data in the context of the sandbox neither leads to measures or decisions affecting the data subjects nor does it affect the application of their rights laid down in Union law on the protection of personal data;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) any personal data processed in the context of the sandbox are protected by means of appropriate technical and organisational measures and deleted once the participation in the sandbox has terminated or the personal data has reached the end of its retention period;
texts.text: (g) any personal data processed in the context of the sandbox are protected by means of appropriate technical and organisational measures and deleted once the participation in the sandbox has terminated or the personal data has reached the end of its retention period;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) the logs of the processing of personal data in the context of the sandbox are kept for the duration of the participation in the sandbox, unless provided otherwise by Union or national law;
texts.text: (h) the logs of the processing of personal data in the context of the sandbox are kept for the duration of the participation in the sandbox, unless provided otherwise by Union or national law;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) a complete and detailed description of the process and rationale behind the training, testing and validation of the AI system is kept together with the testing results as part of the technical documentation referred to in Annex IV;
texts.text: (i) a complete and detailed description of the process and rationale behind the training, testing and validation of the AI system is kept together with the testing results as part of the technical documentation referred to in Annex IV;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) a short summary of the AI project developed in the sandbox, its objectives and expected results is published on the website of the competent authorities; this obligation shall not cover sensitive operational data in relation to the activities of law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities.
texts.text: (j) a short summary of the AI project developed in the sandbox, its objectives and expected results is published on the website of the competent authorities; this obligation shall not cover sensitive operational data in relation to the activities of law enforcement, border control, immigration or asylum authorities.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: For the purposes of the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, including safeguarding against and preventing threats to public security, under the control and responsibility of law enforcement authorities, the processing of personal data in AI regulatory sandboxes shall be based on a specific Union or national law and subject to the same cumulative conditions as referred to in paragraph 1.
texts.text: For the purposes of the prevention, investigation, detection or prosecution of criminal offences or the execution of criminal penalties, including safeguarding against and preventing threats to public security, under the control and responsibility of law enforcement authorities, the processing of personal data in AI regulatory sandboxes shall be based on a specific Union or national law and subject to the same cumulative conditions as referred to in paragraph 1.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Paragraph 1 is without prejudice to Union or national law which excludes processing of personal data for other purposes than those explicitly mentioned in that law, as well as to Union or national law laying down the basis for the processing of personal data which is necessary for the purpose of developing, testing or training of innovative AI systems or any other legal basis, in compliance with Union law on the protection of personal data.
texts.text: Paragraph 1 is without prejudice to Union or national law which excludes processing of personal data for other purposes than those explicitly mentioned in that law, as well as to Union or national law laying down the basis for the processing of personal data which is necessary for the purpose of developing, testing or training of innovative AI systems or any other legal basis, in compliance with Union law on the protection of personal data.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  60
texts.text: Article  60

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Testing of high-risk AI systems in real world conditions outside AI regulatory sandboxes
texts.text: Testing of high-risk AI systems in real world conditions outside AI regulatory sandboxes

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Testing of high-risk AI systems in real world conditions outside AI regulatory sandboxes may be conducted by providers or prospective providers of high-risk AI systems listed in Annex III, in accordance with this Article and the real-world testing plan referred to in this Article, without prejudice to the prohibitions under Article 5.
texts.text: Testing of high-risk AI systems in real world conditions outside AI regulatory sandboxes may be conducted by providers or prospective providers of high-risk AI systems listed in Annex III, in accordance with this Article and the real-world testing plan referred to in this Article, without prejudice to the prohibitions under Article 5.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Commission shall, by means of implementing acts, specify the detailed elements of the real-world testing plan. Those implementing acts shall be  adopted  in  accordance  with  the  examination procedure  referred  to in  Article  98(2).
texts.text: The Commission shall, by means of implementing acts, specify the detailed elements of the real-world testing plan. Those implementing acts shall be  adopted  in  accordance  with  the  examination procedure  referred  to in  Article  98(2).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This paragraph shall be without prejudice to Union or national law on the testing in real world conditions of high-risk AI systems  related  to  products  covered  by  Union  harmonisation  legislation  listed  in  Annex  I.
texts.text: This paragraph shall be without prejudice to Union or national law on the testing in real world conditions of high-risk AI systems  related  to  products  covered  by  Union  harmonisation  legislation  listed  in  Annex  I.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Providers or prospective providers may conduct testing of high-risk AI systems referred to in Annex III in real world conditions at any time before the placing on the market or the putting into service of the AI system on their own or in partnership with one or more deployers or prospective deployers.
texts.text: Providers or prospective providers may conduct testing of high-risk AI systems referred to in Annex III in real world conditions at any time before the placing on the market or the putting into service of the AI system on their own or in partnership with one or more deployers or prospective deployers.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The testing of high-risk AI systems in real world conditions under this Article shall be without prejudice to any ethical review that is required by Union or national law.
texts.text: The testing of high-risk AI systems in real world conditions under this Article shall be without prejudice to any ethical review that is required by Union or national law.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Providers or prospective providers may conduct the testing in real world conditions only where all of the following conditions are met:
texts.text: Providers or prospective providers may conduct the testing in real world conditions only where all of the following conditions are met:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the provider or prospective provider has drawn up a real-world testing plan and submitted it to the market surveillance authority in the Member State where the testing in real world conditions is to be conducted;
texts.text: (a) the provider or prospective provider has drawn up a real-world testing plan and submitted it to the market surveillance authority in the Member State where the testing in real world conditions is to be conducted;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the market surveillance authority in the Member State where the testing in real world conditions is to be conducted has approved the testing in real world conditions and the real-world testing plan; where the market surveillance authority has not provided an answer within 30 days, the testing in real world conditions and the real-world testing plan shall be understood to have been approved; where national law does not provide for a tacit approval, the testing in real world conditions shall remain subject to an authorisation;
texts.text: (b) the market surveillance authority in the Member State where the testing in real world conditions is to be conducted has approved the testing in real world conditions and the real-world testing plan; where the market surveillance authority has not provided an answer within 30 days, the testing in real world conditions and the real-world testing plan shall be understood to have been approved; where national law does not provide for a tacit approval, the testing in real world conditions shall remain subject to an authorisation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the provider or prospective provider, with the exception of providers or prospective providers of high-risk AI systems referred to in points 1, 6 and 7 of Annex III in the areas of law enforcement, migration, asylum and border control management, and high-risk AI systems referred to in point 2 of Annex III has registered the testing in real world conditions in accordance with Article 71(4) with a Union-wide unique single identification number and with the information specified in Annex IX; the provider or prospective provider of high-risk AI systems referred to in points 1, 6 and 7 of Annex III in the areas of law enforcement, migration, asylum and border control management, has registered the testing in real-world conditions in the secure non-public section of the EU database according to Article 49(4), point (d), with a Union-wide unique single identification number and with the information specified therein; the provider or prospective provider of high-risk AI systems referred to in point 2 of Annex III has registered the testing in real-world conditions in accordance with Article 49(5);
texts.text: (c) the provider or prospective provider, with the exception of providers or prospective providers of high-risk AI systems referred to in points 1, 6 and 7 of Annex III in the areas of law enforcement, migration, asylum and border control management, and high-risk AI systems referred to in point 2 of Annex III has registered the testing in real world conditions in accordance with Article 71(4) with a Union-wide unique single identification number and with the information specified in Annex IX; the provider or prospective provider of high-risk AI systems referred to in points 1, 6 and 7 of Annex III in the areas of law enforcement, migration, asylum and border control management, has registered the testing in real-world conditions in the secure non-public section of the EU database according to Article 49(4), point (d), with a Union-wide unique single identification number and with the information specified therein; the provider or prospective provider of high-risk AI systems referred to in point 2 of Annex III has registered the testing in real-world conditions in accordance with Article 49(5);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the provider or prospective provider conducting the testing in real world conditions is established in the Union or has appointed a legal representative who is established in the Union;
texts.text: (d) the provider or prospective provider conducting the testing in real world conditions is established in the Union or has appointed a legal representative who is established in the Union;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) data collected and processed for the purpose of the testing in real world conditions shall be transferred to third countries only provided that appropriate and applicable safeguards under Union law are implemented;
texts.text: (e) data collected and processed for the purpose of the testing in real world conditions shall be transferred to third countries only provided that appropriate and applicable safeguards under Union law are implemented;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) the testing in real world conditions does not last longer than necessary to achieve its objectives and in any case not longer than six months, which may be extended for an additional period of six months, subject to prior notification by the provider or prospective provider to the market surveillance authority, accompanied by an explanation of the need for such an extension;
texts.text: (f) the testing in real world conditions does not last longer than necessary to achieve its objectives and in any case not longer than six months, which may be extended for an additional period of six months, subject to prior notification by the provider or prospective provider to the market surveillance authority, accompanied by an explanation of the need for such an extension;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) the subjects of the testing in real world conditions who are persons belonging to vulnerable groups due to their age or disability, are appropriately protected;
texts.text: (g) the subjects of the testing in real world conditions who are persons belonging to vulnerable groups due to their age or disability, are appropriately protected;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) where a provider or prospective provider organises the testing in real world conditions in cooperation with one or more deployers or prospective deployers, the latter have been informed of all aspects of the testing that are relevant to their decision to participate, and given the relevant instructions for use of the AI system referred to in Article 13; the provider or prospective provider and the deployer or prospective deployer shall conclude an agreement specifying their roles and responsibilities with a view to ensuring compliance with the provisions for testing in real world conditions under this Regulation and under other applicable Union and national law;
texts.text: (h) where a provider or prospective provider organises the testing in real world conditions in cooperation with one or more deployers or prospective deployers, the latter have been informed of all aspects of the testing that are relevant to their decision to participate, and given the relevant instructions for use of the AI system referred to in Article 13; the provider or prospective provider and the deployer or prospective deployer shall conclude an agreement specifying their roles and responsibilities with a view to ensuring compliance with the provisions for testing in real world conditions under this Regulation and under other applicable Union and national law;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) the subjects of the testing in real world conditions have given informed consent in accordance with Article 61, or in the case of law enforcement, where the seeking of informed consent would prevent the AI system from being tested, the testing itself and the outcome of the testing in the real world conditions shall not have any negative effect on the subjects, and their personal data shall be deleted after the test is performed;
texts.text: (i) the subjects of the testing in real world conditions have given informed consent in accordance with Article 61, or in the case of law enforcement, where the seeking of informed consent would prevent the AI system from being tested, the testing itself and the outcome of the testing in the real world conditions shall not have any negative effect on the subjects, and their personal data shall be deleted after the test is performed;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) the testing in real world conditions is effectively overseen by the provider or prospective provider, as well as by deployers or prospective deployers through persons who are suitably qualified in the relevant field and have the necessary capacity, training and authority to perform their tasks;
texts.text: (j) the testing in real world conditions is effectively overseen by the provider or prospective provider, as well as by deployers or prospective deployers through persons who are suitably qualified in the relevant field and have the necessary capacity, training and authority to perform their tasks;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (k) the predictions, recommendations or decisions of the AI system can be effectively reversed and disregarded.
texts.text: (k) the predictions, recommendations or decisions of the AI system can be effectively reversed and disregarded.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Any subjects of the testing in real world conditions, or their legally designated representative, as appropriate, may, without any resulting detriment and without having to provide any justification, withdraw from the testing at any time by revoking their informed consent and may request the immediate and permanent deletion of their personal data. The withdrawal of the informed consent shall not affect the activities already carried out.
texts.text: Any subjects of the testing in real world conditions, or their legally designated representative, as appropriate, may, without any resulting detriment and without having to provide any justification, withdraw from the testing at any time by revoking their informed consent and may request the immediate and permanent deletion of their personal data. The withdrawal of the informed consent shall not affect the activities already carried out.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In accordance with Article 75, Member States shall confer on their market surveillance authorities the powers of requiring providers and prospective providers to provide information, of carrying out unannounced remote or on-site inspections, and of performing checks on the conduct of the testing in real world conditions and the related high-risk AI systems. Market surveillance authorities shall use those powers to ensure the safe development of testing in real world conditions.
texts.text: In accordance with Article 75, Member States shall confer on their market surveillance authorities the powers of requiring providers and prospective providers to provide information, of carrying out unannounced remote or on-site inspections, and of performing checks on the conduct of the testing in real world conditions and the related high-risk AI systems. Market surveillance authorities shall use those powers to ensure the safe development of testing in real world conditions.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Any serious incident identified in the course of the testing in real world conditions shall be reported to the national market surveillance authority in accordance with Article 73. The provider or prospective provider shall adopt immediate mitigation measures or, failing that, shall suspend the testing in real world conditions until such mitigation takes place, or otherwise terminate it. The provider or prospective provider shall establish a procedure for the prompt recall of the AI system upon such termination of the testing in real world conditions.
texts.text: Any serious incident identified in the course of the testing in real world conditions shall be reported to the national market surveillance authority in accordance with Article 73. The provider or prospective provider shall adopt immediate mitigation measures or, failing that, shall suspend the testing in real world conditions until such mitigation takes place, or otherwise terminate it. The provider or prospective provider shall establish a procedure for the prompt recall of the AI system upon such termination of the testing in real world conditions.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Providers or prospective providers shall notify the national market surveillance authority in the Member State where the testing in real world conditions is to be conducted of the suspension or termination of the testing in real world conditions and of the final outcomes.
texts.text: Providers or prospective providers shall notify the national market surveillance authority in the Member State where the testing in real world conditions is to be conducted of the suspension or termination of the testing in real world conditions and of the final outcomes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The provider or prospective provider shall be liable under applicable Union and national liability law for any damage caused in the course of their testing in real world conditions.
texts.text: The provider or prospective provider shall be liable under applicable Union and national liability law for any damage caused in the course of their testing in real world conditions.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  61
texts.text: Article  61

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Informed consent to participate in testing in real world conditions outside AI regulatory sandboxes
texts.text: Informed consent to participate in testing in real world conditions outside AI regulatory sandboxes

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: For the purpose of testing in real world conditions under Article 60, freely-given informed consent shall be obtained from the subjects of testing prior to their participation in such testing and after their having been duly informed with concise, clear, relevant, and understandable information regarding:
texts.text: For the purpose of testing in real world conditions under Article 60, freely-given informed consent shall be obtained from the subjects of testing prior to their participation in such testing and after their having been duly informed with concise, clear, relevant, and understandable information regarding:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the nature and objectives of the testing in real world conditions and the possible inconvenience that may be linked to their participation;
texts.text: (a) the nature and objectives of the testing in real world conditions and the possible inconvenience that may be linked to their participation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the conditions under which the testing in real world conditions is to be conducted, including the expected duration of the subject or subjects' participation;
texts.text: (b) the conditions under which the testing in real world conditions is to be conducted, including the expected duration of the subject or subjects' participation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) their rights, and the guarantees regarding their participation, in particular their right to refuse to participate in, and the right to withdraw from, testing in real world conditions at any time without any resulting detriment and without having to provide any justification;
texts.text: (c) their rights, and the guarantees regarding their participation, in particular their right to refuse to participate in, and the right to withdraw from, testing in real world conditions at any time without any resulting detriment and without having to provide any justification;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the arrangements for requesting the reversal or the disregarding of the predictions, recommendations or decisions of the AI system;
texts.text: (d) the arrangements for requesting the reversal or the disregarding of the predictions, recommendations or decisions of the AI system;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) the Union-wide unique single identification number of the testing in real world conditions in accordance with Article 60(4) point (c), and the contact details of the provider or its legal representative from whom further information can be obtained.
texts.text: (e) the Union-wide unique single identification number of the testing in real world conditions in accordance with Article 60(4) point (c), and the contact details of the provider or its legal representative from whom further information can be obtained.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The informed consent shall be dated and documented and a copy shall be given to the subjects of testing or their legal representative.
texts.text: The informed consent shall be dated and documented and a copy shall be given to the subjects of testing or their legal representative.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  62
texts.text: Article  62

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Measures for  providers and deployers, in particular SMEs, including start-ups
texts.text: Measures for  providers and deployers, in particular SMEs, including start-ups

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member States shall undertake the following actions:
texts.text: Member States shall undertake the following actions:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) provide SMEs, including start-ups, having a registered office or a branch in the Union, with priority access to the AI regulatory sandboxes, to the extent that they fulfil the eligibility conditions and selection criteria; the priority access shall not preclude other SMEs, including start-ups, other than those referred to in this paragraph from access to the AI regulatory sandbox, provided that they also fulfil the eligibility conditions and selection criteria;
texts.text: (a) provide SMEs, including start-ups, having a registered office or a branch in the Union, with priority access to the AI regulatory sandboxes, to the extent that they fulfil the eligibility conditions and selection criteria; the priority access shall not preclude other SMEs, including start-ups, other than those referred to in this paragraph from access to the AI regulatory sandbox, provided that they also fulfil the eligibility conditions and selection criteria;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) organise specific awareness raising and training activities on the application of this Regulation tailored to the needs of SMEs including start-ups, deployers and, as appropriate, local public authorities;
texts.text: (b) organise specific awareness raising and training activities on the application of this Regulation tailored to the needs of SMEs including start-ups, deployers and, as appropriate, local public authorities;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) utilise existing dedicated channels and where appropriate, establish new ones for communication with SMEs including start-ups, deployers, other innovators and, as appropriate, local public authorities to provide advice and respond to queries about the implementation of this Regulation, including as regards participation in AI regulatory sandboxes;
texts.text: (c) utilise existing dedicated channels and where appropriate, establish new ones for communication with SMEs including start-ups, deployers, other innovators and, as appropriate, local public authorities to provide advice and respond to queries about the implementation of this Regulation, including as regards participation in AI regulatory sandboxes;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) facilitate the participation of SMEs and other relevant stakeholders in the standardisation development process.
texts.text: (d) facilitate the participation of SMEs and other relevant stakeholders in the standardisation development process.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The specific interests and needs of the SME providers, including start-ups, shall be taken into account when setting the fees for conformity assessment under Article 43, reducing those fees proportionately to their size, market size and other relevant indicators.
texts.text: The specific interests and needs of the SME providers, including start-ups, shall be taken into account when setting the fees for conformity assessment under Article 43, reducing those fees proportionately to their size, market size and other relevant indicators.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The AI Office shall undertake the following actions:
texts.text: The AI Office shall undertake the following actions:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) provide standardised templates for areas covered by this Regulation, as specified by the Board in its request;
texts.text: (a) provide standardised templates for areas covered by this Regulation, as specified by the Board in its request;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) develop and maintain a single information platform providing easy to use information in relation to this Regulation for all operators across the Union;
texts.text: (b) develop and maintain a single information platform providing easy to use information in relation to this Regulation for all operators across the Union;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) organise appropriate communication campaigns to raise awareness about the obligations arising from this Regulation;
texts.text: (c) organise appropriate communication campaigns to raise awareness about the obligations arising from this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) evaluate and promote the convergence of best practices in public procurement procedures in relation to AI systems.
texts.text: (d) evaluate and promote the convergence of best practices in public procurement procedures in relation to AI systems.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  63
texts.text: Article  63

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Derogations for specific operators
texts.text: Derogations for specific operators

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Microenterprises within the meaning of Recommendation 2003/361/EC may comply with certain elements of the quality management system required by Article 17 of this Regulation in a simplified manner, provided that they do not have partner enterprises or linked enterprises within the meaning of that Recommendation. For that purpose, the Commission shall develop guidelines on the elements of the quality management system which may be complied with in a simplified manner considering the needs of microenterprises, without affecting the level of protection or the need for compliance with the requirements in respect of high-risk AI systems.
texts.text: Microenterprises within the meaning of Recommendation 2003/361/EC may comply with certain elements of the quality management system required by Article 17 of this Regulation in a simplified manner, provided that they do not have partner enterprises or linked enterprises within the meaning of that Recommendation. For that purpose, the Commission shall develop guidelines on the elements of the quality management system which may be complied with in a simplified manner considering the needs of microenterprises, without affecting the level of protection or the need for compliance with the requirements in respect of high-risk AI systems.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Paragraph 1 of this Article shall not be interpreted as exempting those operators from fulfilling any other requirements or obligations laid down in this Regulation, including those established in Articles 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 72 and 73.
texts.text: Paragraph 1 of this Article shall not be interpreted as exempting those operators from fulfilling any other requirements or obligations laid down in this Regulation, including those established in Articles 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 72 and 73.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  65
texts.text: Article  65

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Establishment  and structure of  the European Artificial Intelligence Board
texts.text: Establishment  and structure of  the European Artificial Intelligence Board

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A European Artificial Intelligence Board (the 'Board') is hereby established.
texts.text: A European Artificial Intelligence Board (the 'Board') is hereby established.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall be composed of one representative per Member State. The European Data Protection Supervisor shall participate as observer. The AI Office shall also attend the Board's meetings, without taking part in the votes. Other national and Union authorities, bodies or experts may be invited to the meetings by the Board on a case by case basis, where the issues discussed are of relevance for them.
texts.text: The Board shall be composed of one representative per Member State. The European Data Protection Supervisor shall participate as observer. The AI Office shall also attend the Board's meetings, without taking part in the votes. Other national and Union authorities, bodies or experts may be invited to the meetings by the Board on a case by case basis, where the issues discussed are of relevance for them.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each representative shall be designated by their Member State for a period of three years, renewable once.
texts.text: Each representative shall be designated by their Member State for a period of three years, renewable once.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Member States shall ensure that their representatives on the Board:
texts.text: Member States shall ensure that their representatives on the Board:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) have the relevant competences and powers in their Member State so as to contribute actively to the achievement of the Board's tasks referred to in Article 66;
texts.text: (a) have the relevant competences and powers in their Member State so as to contribute actively to the achievement of the Board's tasks referred to in Article 66;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) are designated as a single contact point vis-à-vis the Board and, where appropriate, taking into account Member States' needs, as a single contact point for stakeholders;
texts.text: (b) are designated as a single contact point vis-à-vis the Board and, where appropriate, taking into account Member States' needs, as a single contact point for stakeholders;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) are empowered to facilitate consistency and coordination between national competent authorities in their Member State as regards the implementation of this Regulation, including through the collection of relevant data and information for the purpose of fulfilling their tasks on the Board.
texts.text: (c) are empowered to facilitate consistency and coordination between national competent authorities in their Member State as regards the implementation of this Regulation, including through the collection of relevant data and information for the purpose of fulfilling their tasks on the Board.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The designated representatives of the Member States shall adopt the Board's rules of procedure by a two-thirds majority. The rules of procedure shall, in particular, lay down procedures for the selection process, the duration of the mandate of, and specifications of the tasks of, the Chair, detailed arrangements for voting, and the organisation of the Board's activities and those of its sub-groups.
texts.text: The designated representatives of the Member States shall adopt the Board's rules of procedure by a two-thirds majority. The rules of procedure shall, in particular, lay down procedures for the selection process, the duration of the mandate of, and specifications of the tasks of, the Chair, detailed arrangements for voting, and the organisation of the Board's activities and those of its sub-groups.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall establish two standing sub-groups to provide a platform for cooperation and exchange among market surveillance authorities and notifying authorities about issues related to market surveillance and notified bodies respectively.
texts.text: The Board shall establish two standing sub-groups to provide a platform for cooperation and exchange among market surveillance authorities and notifying authorities about issues related to market surveillance and notified bodies respectively.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  standing  sub-group  for  market  surveillance  should  act  as  the  administrative  cooperation  group  (ADCO)  for  this Regulation within  the  meaning  of  Article  30  of  Regulation  (EU)  2019/1020.
texts.text: The  standing  sub-group  for  market  surveillance  should  act  as  the  administrative  cooperation  group  (ADCO)  for  this Regulation within  the  meaning  of  Article  30  of  Regulation  (EU)  2019/1020.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  Board  may  establish  other  standing  or  temporary  sub-groups  as  appropriate  for  the  purpose  of  examining  specific issues. Where appropriate, representatives of the advisory forum referred to in Article 67 may be invited to such sub-groups or  to  specific  meetings  of  those  subgroups  as  observers.
texts.text: The  Board  may  establish  other  standing  or  temporary  sub-groups  as  appropriate  for  the  purpose  of  examining  specific issues. Where appropriate, representatives of the advisory forum referred to in Article 67 may be invited to such sub-groups or  to  specific  meetings  of  those  subgroups  as  observers.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall be organised and operated so as to safeguard the objectivity and impartiality of its activities.
texts.text: The Board shall be organised and operated so as to safeguard the objectivity and impartiality of its activities.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Board shall be chaired by one of the representatives of the Member States. The AI Office shall provide the secretariat for the Board, convene the meetings upon request of the Chair, and prepare the agenda in accordance with the tasks of the Board pursuant to this Regulation and its rules of procedure.
texts.text: The Board shall be chaired by one of the representatives of the Member States. The AI Office shall provide the secretariat for the Board, convene the meetings upon request of the Chair, and prepare the agenda in accordance with the tasks of the Board pursuant to this Regulation and its rules of procedure.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  66
texts.text: Article  66

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Tasks  of  the  Board
texts.text: Tasks  of  the  Board

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Board shall advise and assist the Commission and the Member States in order to facilitate the consistent and effective application  of  this  Regulation.  To  that  end,  the  Board  may  in  particular:
texts.text: The Board shall advise and assist the Commission and the Member States in order to facilitate the consistent and effective application  of  this  Regulation.  To  that  end,  the  Board  may  in  particular:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) contribute to the coordination among national competent authorities responsible for the application of this Regulation and, in cooperation with and subject to the agreement of the market surveillance authorities concerned, support joint activities of market surveillance authorities referred to in Article 74(11);
texts.text: (a) contribute to the coordination among national competent authorities responsible for the application of this Regulation and, in cooperation with and subject to the agreement of the market surveillance authorities concerned, support joint activities of market surveillance authorities referred to in Article 74(11);

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) collect and share technical and regulatory expertise and best practices among Member States;
texts.text: (b) collect and share technical and regulatory expertise and best practices among Member States;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) provide advice on the implementation of this Regulation, in particular as regards the enforcement of rules on general-purpose AI models;
texts.text: (c) provide advice on the implementation of this Regulation, in particular as regards the enforcement of rules on general-purpose AI models;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) contribute to the harmonisation of administrative practices in the Member States, including in relation to the derogation from the conformity assessment procedures referred to in Article 46, the functioning of AI regulatory sandboxes, and testing in real world conditions referred to in Articles 57, 59 and 60;
texts.text: (d) contribute to the harmonisation of administrative practices in the Member States, including in relation to the derogation from the conformity assessment procedures referred to in Article 46, the functioning of AI regulatory sandboxes, and testing in real world conditions referred to in Articles 57, 59 and 60;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) at the request of the Commission or on its own initiative, issue recommendations and written opinions on any relevant matters related to the implementation of this Regulation and to its consistent and effective application, including:
texts.text: (e) at the request of the Commission or on its own initiative, issue recommendations and written opinions on any relevant matters related to the implementation of this Regulation and to its consistent and effective application, including:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) on the development and application of codes of conduct and codes of practice pursuant to this Regulation, as well as of the Commission's guidelines;
texts.text: (i) on the development and application of codes of conduct and codes of practice pursuant to this Regulation, as well as of the Commission's guidelines;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (ii) the evaluation and review of this Regulation pursuant to Article 112, including as regards the serious incident reports referred to in Article 73, and the functioning of the EU database referred to in Article 71, the preparation of the delegated or implementing acts, and as regards possible alignments of this Regulation with the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I;
texts.text: (ii) the evaluation and review of this Regulation pursuant to Article 112, including as regards the serious incident reports referred to in Article 73, and the functioning of the EU database referred to in Article 71, the preparation of the delegated or implementing acts, and as regards possible alignments of this Regulation with the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (iii) on technical specifications or existing standards regarding the requirements set out in Chapter III, Section 2;
texts.text: (iii) on technical specifications or existing standards regarding the requirements set out in Chapter III, Section 2;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (iv) on the use of harmonised standards or common specifications referred to in Articles 40 and 41;
texts.text: (iv) on the use of harmonised standards or common specifications referred to in Articles 40 and 41;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (v) trends, such as European global competitiveness in AI, the uptake of AI in the Union, and the development of digital skills;
texts.text: (v) trends, such as European global competitiveness in AI, the uptake of AI in the Union, and the development of digital skills;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (vi) trends on the evolving typology of AI value chains, in particular on the resulting implications in terms of accountability;
texts.text: (vi) trends on the evolving typology of AI value chains, in particular on the resulting implications in terms of accountability;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (vii) on the potential need for amendment to Annex III in accordance with Article 7, and on the potential need for possible revision of Article 5 pursuant to Article 112, taking into account relevant available evidence and the latest developments in technology;
texts.text: (vii) on the potential need for amendment to Annex III in accordance with Article 7, and on the potential need for possible revision of Article 5 pursuant to Article 112, taking into account relevant available evidence and the latest developments in technology;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) support the Commission in promoting AI literacy, public awareness and understanding of the benefits, risks, safeguards and rights and obligations in relation to the use of AI systems;
texts.text: (f) support the Commission in promoting AI literacy, public awareness and understanding of the benefits, risks, safeguards and rights and obligations in relation to the use of AI systems;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) facilitate the development of common criteria and a shared understanding among market operators and competent authorities of the relevant concepts provided for in this Regulation, including by contributing to the development of benchmarks;
texts.text: (g) facilitate the development of common criteria and a shared understanding among market operators and competent authorities of the relevant concepts provided for in this Regulation, including by contributing to the development of benchmarks;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (h) cooperate, as appropriate, with other Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies, as well as relevant Union expert groups and networks, in particular in the fields of product safety, cybersecurity, competition, digital and media services, financial services, consumer protection, data and fundamental rights protection;
texts.text: (h) cooperate, as appropriate, with other Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies, as well as relevant Union expert groups and networks, in particular in the fields of product safety, cybersecurity, competition, digital and media services, financial services, consumer protection, data and fundamental rights protection;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) contribute to effective cooperation with the competent authorities of third countries and with international organisations;
texts.text: (i) contribute to effective cooperation with the competent authorities of third countries and with international organisations;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (j) assist national competent authorities and the Commission in developing the organisational and technical expertise required for the implementation of this Regulation, including by contributing to the assessment of training needs for staff of Member States involved in implementing this Regulation;
texts.text: (j) assist national competent authorities and the Commission in developing the organisational and technical expertise required for the implementation of this Regulation, including by contributing to the assessment of training needs for staff of Member States involved in implementing this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (k) assist the AI Office in supporting national competent authorities in the establishment and development of AI regulatory sandboxes, and facilitate cooperation and information-sharing among AI regulatory sandboxes;
texts.text: (k) assist the AI Office in supporting national competent authorities in the establishment and development of AI regulatory sandboxes, and facilitate cooperation and information-sharing among AI regulatory sandboxes;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (l) contribute to, and provide relevant advice on, the development of guidance documents;
texts.text: (l) contribute to, and provide relevant advice on, the development of guidance documents;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (m) advise the Commission in relation to international matters on AI;
texts.text: (m) advise the Commission in relation to international matters on AI;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (n) provide opinions to the Commission on the qualified alerts regarding general-purpose AI models;
texts.text: (n) provide opinions to the Commission on the qualified alerts regarding general-purpose AI models;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (o) receive opinions by the Member States on qualified alerts regarding general-purpose AI models, and on national experiences and practices on the monitoring and enforcement of AI systems, in particular systems integrating the general-purpose AI models.
texts.text: (o) receive opinions by the Member States on qualified alerts regarding general-purpose AI models, and on national experiences and practices on the monitoring and enforcement of AI systems, in particular systems integrating the general-purpose AI models.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  67
texts.text: Article  67

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Advisory forum
texts.text: Advisory forum

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: An advisory forum shall be established to provide technical expertise and advise the Board and the Commission, and to contribute to their tasks under this Regulation.
texts.text: An advisory forum shall be established to provide technical expertise and advise the Board and the Commission, and to contribute to their tasks under this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The membership of the advisory forum shall represent a balanced selection of stakeholders, including industry, start-ups, SMEs, civil society and academia. The membership of the advisory forum shall be balanced with regard to commercial and non-commercial interests and, within the category of commercial interests, with regard to SMEs and other undertakings.
texts.text: The membership of the advisory forum shall represent a balanced selection of stakeholders, including industry, start-ups, SMEs, civil society and academia. The membership of the advisory forum shall be balanced with regard to commercial and non-commercial interests and, within the category of commercial interests, with regard to SMEs and other undertakings.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall appoint the members of the advisory forum, in accordance with the criteria set out in paragraph 2, from amongst stakeholders with recognised expertise in the field of AI.
texts.text: The Commission shall appoint the members of the advisory forum, in accordance with the criteria set out in paragraph 2, from amongst stakeholders with recognised expertise in the field of AI.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The term of office of the members of the advisory forum shall be two years, which may be extended by up to no more than four years.
texts.text: The term of office of the members of the advisory forum shall be two years, which may be extended by up to no more than four years.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Fundamental Rights Agency, ENISA, the European Committee for Standardization (CEN), the European Committee for Electrotechnical Standardization (CENELEC), and the European Telecommunications Standards Institute (ETSI) shall be permanent members of the advisory forum.
texts.text: The Fundamental Rights Agency, ENISA, the European Committee for Standardization (CEN), the European Committee for Electrotechnical Standardization (CENELEC), and the European Telecommunications Standards Institute (ETSI) shall be permanent members of the advisory forum.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The advisory forum shall draw up its rules of procedure. It shall elect two co-chairs from among its members, in accordance with criteria set out in paragraph 2. The term of office of the co-chairs shall be two years, renewable once.
texts.text: The advisory forum shall draw up its rules of procedure. It shall elect two co-chairs from among its members, in accordance with criteria set out in paragraph 2. The term of office of the co-chairs shall be two years, renewable once.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The advisory forum shall hold meetings at least twice a year. The advisory forum may invite experts and other stakeholders to its meetings.
texts.text: The advisory forum shall hold meetings at least twice a year. The advisory forum may invite experts and other stakeholders to its meetings.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The advisory forum may prepare opinions, recommendations and written contributions at the request of the Board or the Commission.
texts.text: The advisory forum may prepare opinions, recommendations and written contributions at the request of the Board or the Commission.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The advisory forum may establish standing or temporary sub-groups as appropriate for the purpose of examining specific questions related to the objectives of this Regulation.
texts.text: The advisory forum may establish standing or temporary sub-groups as appropriate for the purpose of examining specific questions related to the objectives of this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The advisory forum shall prepare an annual report on its activities. That report shall be made publicly available.
texts.text: The advisory forum shall prepare an annual report on its activities. That report shall be made publicly available.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  68
texts.text: Article  68

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Scientific  panel  of  independent  experts
texts.text: Scientific  panel  of  independent  experts

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall, by means of an implementing act, make provisions on the establishment of a scientific panel of independent experts (the 'scientific panel') intended to support the enforcement activities under this Regulation. That implementing act shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 98(2).
texts.text: The Commission shall, by means of an implementing act, make provisions on the establishment of a scientific panel of independent experts (the 'scientific panel') intended to support the enforcement activities under this Regulation. That implementing act shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 98(2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The scientific panel shall consist of experts selected by the Commission on the basis of up-to-date scientific or technical expertise in the field of AI necessary for the tasks set out in paragraph 3, and shall be able to demonstrate meeting all of the following conditions:
texts.text: The scientific panel shall consist of experts selected by the Commission on the basis of up-to-date scientific or technical expertise in the field of AI necessary for the tasks set out in paragraph 3, and shall be able to demonstrate meeting all of the following conditions:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) having particular expertise and competence and scientific or technical expertise in the field of AI;
texts.text: (a) having particular expertise and competence and scientific or technical expertise in the field of AI;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) independence from any provider of AI systems or general-purpose AI models;
texts.text: (b) independence from any provider of AI systems or general-purpose AI models;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) an ability to carry out activities diligently, accurately and objectively.
texts.text: (c) an ability to carry out activities diligently, accurately and objectively.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Commission, in consultation with the Board, shall determine the number of experts on the panel in accordance with the  required  needs  and  shall  ensure  fair  gender  and  geographical  representation.
texts.text: The Commission, in consultation with the Board, shall determine the number of experts on the panel in accordance with the  required  needs  and  shall  ensure  fair  gender  and  geographical  representation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The scientific panel shall advise and support the AI Office, in particular with regard to the following tasks:
texts.text: The scientific panel shall advise and support the AI Office, in particular with regard to the following tasks:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) supporting the implementation and enforcement of this Regulation as regards general-purpose AI models and systems, in particular by:
texts.text: (a) supporting the implementation and enforcement of this Regulation as regards general-purpose AI models and systems, in particular by:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) alerting the AI Office of possible systemic risks at Union level of general-purpose AI models, in accordance with Article 90;
texts.text: (i) alerting the AI Office of possible systemic risks at Union level of general-purpose AI models, in accordance with Article 90;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (ii) contributing to the development of tools and methodologies for evaluating capabilities of general-purpose AI models and systems, including through benchmarks;
texts.text: (ii) contributing to the development of tools and methodologies for evaluating capabilities of general-purpose AI models and systems, including through benchmarks;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (iii) providing advice on the classification of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk;
texts.text: (iii) providing advice on the classification of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (iv) providing advice on the classification of various general-purpose AI models and systems;
texts.text: (iv) providing advice on the classification of various general-purpose AI models and systems;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (v) contributing to the development of tools and templates;
texts.text: (v) contributing to the development of tools and templates;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) supporting the work of market surveillance authorities, at their request;
texts.text: (b) supporting the work of market surveillance authorities, at their request;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) supporting cross-border market surveillance activities as referred to in Article 74(11), without prejudice to the powers of market surveillance authorities;
texts.text: (c) supporting cross-border market surveillance activities as referred to in Article 74(11), without prejudice to the powers of market surveillance authorities;

texts.label: list_item
