texts.orig: Where a market surveillance authority has sufficient reason to consider that an AI system classified by the provider as non-high-risk pursuant to Article 6(3) is indeed high-risk, the market surveillance authority shall carry out an evaluation of the AI system concerned in respect of its classification as a high-risk AI system based on the conditions set out in Article 6(3) and the Commission guidelines.
texts.text: Where a market surveillance authority has sufficient reason to consider that an AI system classified by the provider as non-high-risk pursuant to Article 6(3) is indeed high-risk, the market surveillance authority shall carry out an evaluation of the AI system concerned in respect of its classification as a high-risk AI system based on the conditions set out in Article 6(3) and the Commission guidelines.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where, in the course of that evaluation, the market surveillance authority finds that the AI system concerned is high-risk, it shall without undue delay require the relevant provider to take all necessary actions to bring the AI system into compliance with the requirements and obligations laid down in this Regulation, as well as take appropriate corrective action within a period the market surveillance authority may prescribe.
texts.text: Where, in the course of that evaluation, the market surveillance authority finds that the AI system concerned is high-risk, it shall without undue delay require the relevant provider to take all necessary actions to bring the AI system into compliance with the requirements and obligations laid down in this Regulation, as well as take appropriate corrective action within a period the market surveillance authority may prescribe.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the market surveillance authority considers that the use of the AI system concerned is not restricted to its national territory, it shall inform the Commission and the other Member States without undue delay of the results of the evaluation and of the actions which it has required the provider to take.
texts.text: Where the market surveillance authority considers that the use of the AI system concerned is not restricted to its national territory, it shall inform the Commission and the other Member States without undue delay of the results of the evaluation and of the actions which it has required the provider to take.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The provider shall ensure that all necessary action is taken to bring the AI system into compliance with the requirements and obligations laid down in this Regulation. Where the provider of an AI system concerned does not bring the AI system into compliance with those requirements and obligations within the period referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article, the provider shall be subject to fines in accordance with Article 99.
texts.text: The provider shall ensure that all necessary action is taken to bring the AI system into compliance with the requirements and obligations laid down in this Regulation. Where the provider of an AI system concerned does not bring the AI system into compliance with those requirements and obligations within the period referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article, the provider shall be subject to fines in accordance with Article 99.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The provider shall ensure that all appropriate corrective action is taken in respect of all the AI systems concerned that it has made available on the Union market.
texts.text: The provider shall ensure that all appropriate corrective action is taken in respect of all the AI systems concerned that it has made available on the Union market.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the provider of the AI system concerned does not take adequate corrective action within the period referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article, Article 79(5) to (9) shall apply.
texts.text: Where the provider of the AI system concerned does not take adequate corrective action within the period referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article, Article 79(5) to (9) shall apply.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where, in the course of the evaluation pursuant to paragraph 1 of this Article, the market surveillance authority establishes that the AI system was misclassified by the provider as non-high-risk in order to circumvent the application of requirements in Chapter III, Section 2, the provider shall be subject to fines in accordance with Article 99.
texts.text: Where, in the course of the evaluation pursuant to paragraph 1 of this Article, the market surveillance authority establishes that the AI system was misclassified by the provider as non-high-risk in order to circumvent the application of requirements in Chapter III, Section 2, the provider shall be subject to fines in accordance with Article 99.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: In exercising their power to monitor the application of this Article, and in accordance with Article 11 of Regulation (EU) 2019/1020, market surveillance authorities may perform appropriate checks, taking into account in particular information stored in the EU database referred to in Article 71 of this Regulation.
texts.text: In exercising their power to monitor the application of this Article, and in accordance with Article 11 of Regulation (EU) 2019/1020, market surveillance authorities may perform appropriate checks, taking into account in particular information stored in the EU database referred to in Article 71 of this Regulation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  81
texts.text: Article  81

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Union safeguard procedure
texts.text: Union safeguard procedure

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where, within three months of receipt of the notification referred to in Article 79(5), or within 30 days in the case of non-compliance with the prohibition of the AI practices referred to in Article 5, objections are raised by the market surveillance authority of a Member State to a measure taken by another market surveillance authority, or where the Commission considers the measure to be contrary to Union law, the Commission shall without undue delay enter into consultation with the market surveillance authority of the relevant Member State and the operator or operators, and shall evaluate the national measure. On the basis of the results of that evaluation, the Commission shall, within six months, or within 60 days in the case of non-compliance with the prohibition of the AI practices referred to in Article 5, starting from the notification referred to in Article 79(5), decide whether the national measure is justified and shall notify its decision to the market surveillance authority of the Member State concerned. The Commission shall also inform all other market surveillance authorities of its decision.
texts.text: Where, within three months of receipt of the notification referred to in Article 79(5), or within 30 days in the case of non-compliance with the prohibition of the AI practices referred to in Article 5, objections are raised by the market surveillance authority of a Member State to a measure taken by another market surveillance authority, or where the Commission considers the measure to be contrary to Union law, the Commission shall without undue delay enter into consultation with the market surveillance authority of the relevant Member State and the operator or operators, and shall evaluate the national measure. On the basis of the results of that evaluation, the Commission shall, within six months, or within 60 days in the case of non-compliance with the prohibition of the AI practices referred to in Article 5, starting from the notification referred to in Article 79(5), decide whether the national measure is justified and shall notify its decision to the market surveillance authority of the Member State concerned. The Commission shall also inform all other market surveillance authorities of its decision.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the Commission considers the measure taken by the relevant Member State to be justified, all Member States shall ensure that they take appropriate restrictive measures in respect of the AI system concerned, such as requiring the withdrawal of the AI system from their market without undue delay, and shall inform the Commission accordingly. Where the Commission considers the national measure to be unjustified, the Member State concerned shall withdraw the measure and shall inform the Commission accordingly.
texts.text: Where the Commission considers the measure taken by the relevant Member State to be justified, all Member States shall ensure that they take appropriate restrictive measures in respect of the AI system concerned, such as requiring the withdrawal of the AI system from their market without undue delay, and shall inform the Commission accordingly. Where the Commission considers the national measure to be unjustified, the Member State concerned shall withdraw the measure and shall inform the Commission accordingly.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the national measure is considered justified and the non-compliance of the AI system is attributed to shortcomings in the harmonised standards or common specifications referred to in Articles 40 and 41 of this Regulation, the Commission shall apply the procedure provided for in Article 11 of Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012.
texts.text: Where the national measure is considered justified and the non-compliance of the AI system is attributed to shortcomings in the harmonised standards or common specifications referred to in Articles 40 and 41 of this Regulation, the Commission shall apply the procedure provided for in Article 11 of Regulation (EU) No 1025/2012.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  82
texts.text: Article  82

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Compliant AI systems which present a risk
texts.text: Compliant AI systems which present a risk

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where, having performed an evaluation under Article 79, after consulting the relevant national public authority referred to in Article 77(1), the market surveillance authority of a Member State finds that although a high-risk AI system complies with this Regulation, it nevertheless presents a risk to the health or safety of persons, to fundamental rights, or to other aspects of public interest protection, it shall require the relevant operator to take all appropriate measures to ensure that the AI system concerned, when placed on the market or put into service, no longer presents that risk without undue delay, within a period it may prescribe.
texts.text: Where, having performed an evaluation under Article 79, after consulting the relevant national public authority referred to in Article 77(1), the market surveillance authority of a Member State finds that although a high-risk AI system complies with this Regulation, it nevertheless presents a risk to the health or safety of persons, to fundamental rights, or to other aspects of public interest protection, it shall require the relevant operator to take all appropriate measures to ensure that the AI system concerned, when placed on the market or put into service, no longer presents that risk without undue delay, within a period it may prescribe.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The provider or other relevant operator shall ensure that corrective action is taken in respect of all the AI systems concerned that it has made available on the Union market within the timeline prescribed by the market surveillance authority of the Member State referred to in paragraph 1.
texts.text: The provider or other relevant operator shall ensure that corrective action is taken in respect of all the AI systems concerned that it has made available on the Union market within the timeline prescribed by the market surveillance authority of the Member State referred to in paragraph 1.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Member States shall immediately inform the Commission and the other Member States of a finding under paragraph 1. That information shall include all available details, in particular the data necessary for the identification of the AI system concerned, the origin and the supply chain of the AI system, the nature of the risk involved and the nature and duration of the national measures taken.
texts.text: The Member States shall immediately inform the Commission and the other Member States of a finding under paragraph 1. That information shall include all available details, in particular the data necessary for the identification of the AI system concerned, the origin and the supply chain of the AI system, the nature of the risk involved and the nature and duration of the national measures taken.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall without undue delay enter into consultation with the Member States concerned and the relevant operators, and shall evaluate the national measures taken. On the basis of the results of that evaluation, the Commission shall decide whether the measure is justified and, where necessary, propose other appropriate measures.
texts.text: The Commission shall without undue delay enter into consultation with the Member States concerned and the relevant operators, and shall evaluate the national measures taken. On the basis of the results of that evaluation, the Commission shall decide whether the measure is justified and, where necessary, propose other appropriate measures.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall immediately communicate its decision to the Member States concerned and to the relevant operators. It shall also inform the other Member States.
texts.text: The Commission shall immediately communicate its decision to the Member States concerned and to the relevant operators. It shall also inform the other Member States.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  83
texts.text: Article  83

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Formal non-compliance
texts.text: Formal non-compliance

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the market surveillance authority of a Member State makes one of the following findings, it shall require the relevant provider to put an end to the non-compliance concerned, within a period it may prescribe:
texts.text: Where the market surveillance authority of a Member State makes one of the following findings, it shall require the relevant provider to put an end to the non-compliance concerned, within a period it may prescribe:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the CE marking has been affixed in violation of Article 48;
texts.text: (a) the CE marking has been affixed in violation of Article 48;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the CE marking has not been affixed;
texts.text: (b) the CE marking has not been affixed;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the EU declaration of conformity referred to in Article 47 has not been drawn up;
texts.text: (c) the EU declaration of conformity referred to in Article 47 has not been drawn up;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the EU declaration of conformity referred to in Article 47 has not been drawn up correctly;
texts.text: (d) the EU declaration of conformity referred to in Article 47 has not been drawn up correctly;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) the registration in the EU database referred to in Article 71 has not been carried out;
texts.text: (e) the registration in the EU database referred to in Article 71 has not been carried out;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) where applicable, no authorised representative has been appointed;
texts.text: (f) where applicable, no authorised representative has been appointed;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (g) technical documentation is not available.
texts.text: (g) technical documentation is not available.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where the non-compliance referred to in paragraph 1 persists, the market surveillance authority of the Member State concerned shall take appropriate and proportionate measures to restrict or prohibit the high-risk AI system being made available on the market or to ensure that it is recalled or withdrawn from the market without delay.
texts.text: Where the non-compliance referred to in paragraph 1 persists, the market surveillance authority of the Member State concerned shall take appropriate and proportionate measures to restrict or prohibit the high-risk AI system being made available on the market or to ensure that it is recalled or withdrawn from the market without delay.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  84
texts.text: Article  84

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Union AI testing support structures
texts.text: Union AI testing support structures

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall designate one or more Union AI testing support structures to perform the tasks listed under Article 21(6) of Regulation (EU) 2019/1020 in the area of AI.
texts.text: The Commission shall designate one or more Union AI testing support structures to perform the tasks listed under Article 21(6) of Regulation (EU) 2019/1020 in the area of AI.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to the tasks referred to in paragraph 1, Union AI testing support structures shall also provide independent technical or scientific advice at the request of the Board, the Commission, or of market surveillance authorities.
texts.text: Without prejudice to the tasks referred to in paragraph 1, Union AI testing support structures shall also provide independent technical or scientific advice at the request of the Board, the Commission, or of market surveillance authorities.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: SECTION 4
texts.text: SECTION 4

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Remedies
texts.text: Remedies

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  85
texts.text: Article  85

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to lodge a complaint with a market surveillance authority
texts.text: Right to lodge a complaint with a market surveillance authority

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Without prejudice to other administrative or judicial remedies, any natural or legal person having grounds to consider that there  has  been  an  infringement  of  the  provisions  of  this  Regulation  may  submit  complaints  to  the  relevant  market surveillance  authority.
texts.text: Without prejudice to other administrative or judicial remedies, any natural or legal person having grounds to consider that there  has  been  an  infringement  of  the  provisions  of  this  Regulation  may  submit  complaints  to  the  relevant  market surveillance  authority.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In accordance with Regulation (EU) 2019/1020, such complaints shall be taken into account for the purpose of conducting market surveillance activities, and shall be handled in line with the dedicated procedures established therefor by the market surveillance  authorities.
texts.text: In accordance with Regulation (EU) 2019/1020, such complaints shall be taken into account for the purpose of conducting market surveillance activities, and shall be handled in line with the dedicated procedures established therefor by the market surveillance  authorities.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  86
texts.text: Article  86

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Right to explanation of  individual decision-making
texts.text: Right to explanation of  individual decision-making

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Any affected person subject to a decision which is taken by the deployer on the basis of the output from a high-risk AI system listed in Annex III, with the exception of systems listed under point 2 thereof, and which produces legal effects or similarly significantly affects that person in a way that they consider to have an adverse impact on their health, safety or fundamental rights shall have the right to obtain from the deployer clear and meaningful explanations of the role of the AI system in the decision-making procedure and the main elements of the decision taken.
texts.text: Any affected person subject to a decision which is taken by the deployer on the basis of the output from a high-risk AI system listed in Annex III, with the exception of systems listed under point 2 thereof, and which produces legal effects or similarly significantly affects that person in a way that they consider to have an adverse impact on their health, safety or fundamental rights shall have the right to obtain from the deployer clear and meaningful explanations of the role of the AI system in the decision-making procedure and the main elements of the decision taken.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Paragraph 1 shall not apply to the use of AI systems for which exceptions from, or restrictions to, the obligation under that paragraph follow from Union or national law in compliance with Union law.
texts.text: Paragraph 1 shall not apply to the use of AI systems for which exceptions from, or restrictions to, the obligation under that paragraph follow from Union or national law in compliance with Union law.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: This Article shall apply only to the extent that the right referred to in paragraph 1 is not otherwise provided for under Union law.
texts.text: This Article shall apply only to the extent that the right referred to in paragraph 1 is not otherwise provided for under Union law.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  87
texts.text: Article  87

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Reporting of  infringements and protection of reporting persons
texts.text: Reporting of  infringements and protection of reporting persons

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Directive  (EU)  2019/1937 shall  apply  to the  reporting  of  infringements  of  this  Regulation  and  the  protection  of  persons reporting  such  infringements.
texts.text: Directive  (EU)  2019/1937 shall  apply  to the  reporting  of  infringements  of  this  Regulation  and  the  protection  of  persons reporting  such  infringements.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: SECTION 5
texts.text: SECTION 5

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Supervision,  investigation,  enforcement  and  monitoring  in  respect  of  providers  of  general-purpose  AI  models
texts.text: Supervision,  investigation,  enforcement  and  monitoring  in  respect  of  providers  of  general-purpose  AI  models

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  88
texts.text: Article  88

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Enforcement of  the obligations of providers of general-purpose AI models
texts.text: Enforcement of  the obligations of providers of general-purpose AI models

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall have exclusive powers to supervise and enforce Chapter V, taking into account the procedural guarantees under Article 94. The Commission shall entrust the implementation of these tasks to the AI Office, without prejudice to the powers of organisation of the Commission and the division of competences between Member States and the Union based on the Treaties.
texts.text: The Commission shall have exclusive powers to supervise and enforce Chapter V, taking into account the procedural guarantees under Article 94. The Commission shall entrust the implementation of these tasks to the AI Office, without prejudice to the powers of organisation of the Commission and the division of competences between Member States and the Union based on the Treaties.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Without prejudice to Article 75(3), market surveillance authorities may request the Commission to exercise the powers laid down in this Section, where that is necessary and proportionate to assist with the fulfilment of their tasks under this Regulation.
texts.text: Without prejudice to Article 75(3), market surveillance authorities may request the Commission to exercise the powers laid down in this Section, where that is necessary and proportionate to assist with the fulfilment of their tasks under this Regulation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  89
texts.text: Article  89

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Monitoring actions
texts.text: Monitoring actions

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: For the purpose of carrying out the tasks assigned to it under this Section, the AI Office may take the necessary actions to monitor the effective implementation and compliance with this Regulation by providers of general-purpose AI models, including their adherence to approved codes of practice.
texts.text: For the purpose of carrying out the tasks assigned to it under this Section, the AI Office may take the necessary actions to monitor the effective implementation and compliance with this Regulation by providers of general-purpose AI models, including their adherence to approved codes of practice.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Downstream providers shall have the right to lodge a complaint alleging an infringement of this Regulation. A complaint shall be duly reasoned and indicate at least:
texts.text: Downstream providers shall have the right to lodge a complaint alleging an infringement of this Regulation. A complaint shall be duly reasoned and indicate at least:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the point of contact of the provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned;
texts.text: (a) the point of contact of the provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) a description of the relevant facts, the provisions of this Regulation concerned, and the reason why the downstream provider considers that the provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned infringed this Regulation;
texts.text: (b) a description of the relevant facts, the provisions of this Regulation concerned, and the reason why the downstream provider considers that the provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned infringed this Regulation;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) any other information that the downstream provider that sent the request considers relevant, including, where appropriate, information gathered on its own initiative.
texts.text: (c) any other information that the downstream provider that sent the request considers relevant, including, where appropriate, information gathered on its own initiative.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  90
texts.text: Article  90

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Alerts of  systemic  risks  by the  scientific  panel
texts.text: Alerts of  systemic  risks  by the  scientific  panel

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The scientific panel may provide a qualified alert to the AI Office where it has reason to suspect that:
texts.text: The scientific panel may provide a qualified alert to the AI Office where it has reason to suspect that:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) a general-purpose AI model poses concrete identifiable risk at Union level; or
texts.text: (a) a general-purpose AI model poses concrete identifiable risk at Union level; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) a general-purpose AI model meets the conditions referred to in Article 51.
texts.text: (b) a general-purpose AI model meets the conditions referred to in Article 51.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Upon such qualified alert, the Commission, through the AI Office and after having informed the Board, may exercise the powers laid down in this Section for the purpose of assessing the matter. The AI Office shall inform the Board of any measure according to Articles 91 to 94.
texts.text: Upon such qualified alert, the Commission, through the AI Office and after having informed the Board, may exercise the powers laid down in this Section for the purpose of assessing the matter. The AI Office shall inform the Board of any measure according to Articles 91 to 94.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A qualified alert shall be duly reasoned and indicate at least:
texts.text: A qualified alert shall be duly reasoned and indicate at least:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the point of contact of the provider of the general-purpose AI model with systemic risk concerned;
texts.text: (a) the point of contact of the provider of the general-purpose AI model with systemic risk concerned;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) a description of the relevant facts and the reasons for the alert by the scientific panel;
texts.text: (b) a description of the relevant facts and the reasons for the alert by the scientific panel;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) any other information that the scientific panel considers to be relevant, including, where appropriate, information gathered on its own initiative.
texts.text: (c) any other information that the scientific panel considers to be relevant, including, where appropriate, information gathered on its own initiative.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  91
texts.text: Article  91

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Power  to request  documentation and information
texts.text: Power  to request  documentation and information

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission may request the provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned to provide the documentation drawn up by the provider in accordance with Articles 53 and 55, or any additional information that is necessary for the purpose of assessing compliance of the provider with this Regulation.
texts.text: The Commission may request the provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned to provide the documentation drawn up by the provider in accordance with Articles 53 and 55, or any additional information that is necessary for the purpose of assessing compliance of the provider with this Regulation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Before sending the request for information, the AI Office may initiate a structured dialogue with the provider of the general-purpose AI model.
texts.text: Before sending the request for information, the AI Office may initiate a structured dialogue with the provider of the general-purpose AI model.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Upon a duly substantiated request from the scientific panel, the Commission may issue a request for information to a provider of a general-purpose AI model, where the access to information is necessary and proportionate for the fulfilment of the tasks of the scientific panel under Article 68(2).
texts.text: Upon a duly substantiated request from the scientific panel, the Commission may issue a request for information to a provider of a general-purpose AI model, where the access to information is necessary and proportionate for the fulfilment of the tasks of the scientific panel under Article 68(2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The request for information shall state the legal basis and the purpose of the request, specify what information is required, set a period within which the information is to be provided, and indicate the fines provided for in Article 101 for supplying incorrect, incomplete or misleading information.
texts.text: The request for information shall state the legal basis and the purpose of the request, specify what information is required, set a period within which the information is to be provided, and indicate the fines provided for in Article 101 for supplying incorrect, incomplete or misleading information.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned, or its representative shall supply the information requested. In the case of legal persons, companies or firms, or where the provider has no legal personality, the persons authorised to represent them by law or by their statutes, shall supply the information requested on behalf of the provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned. Lawyers duly authorised to act may supply information on behalf of their clients. The clients shall nevertheless remain fully responsible if the information supplied is incomplete, incorrect or misleading.
texts.text: The provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned, or its representative shall supply the information requested. In the case of legal persons, companies or firms, or where the provider has no legal personality, the persons authorised to represent them by law or by their statutes, shall supply the information requested on behalf of the provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned. Lawyers duly authorised to act may supply information on behalf of their clients. The clients shall nevertheless remain fully responsible if the information supplied is incomplete, incorrect or misleading.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  92
texts.text: Article  92

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Power  to conduct evaluations
texts.text: Power  to conduct evaluations

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The AI Office, after consulting the Board, may conduct evaluations of the general-purpose AI model concerned:
texts.text: The AI Office, after consulting the Board, may conduct evaluations of the general-purpose AI model concerned:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) to assess compliance of the provider with obligations under this Regulation, where the information gathered pursuant to Article 91 is insufficient; or
texts.text: (a) to assess compliance of the provider with obligations under this Regulation, where the information gathered pursuant to Article 91 is insufficient; or

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) to investigate systemic risks at Union level of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk, in particular following a qualified alert from the scientific panel in accordance with Article 90(1), point (a).
texts.text: (b) to investigate systemic risks at Union level of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk, in particular following a qualified alert from the scientific panel in accordance with Article 90(1), point (a).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission may decide to appoint independent experts to carry out evaluations on its behalf, including from the scientific panel established pursuant to Article 68. Independent experts appointed for this task shall meet the criteria outlined in Article 68(2).
texts.text: The Commission may decide to appoint independent experts to carry out evaluations on its behalf, including from the scientific panel established pursuant to Article 68. Independent experts appointed for this task shall meet the criteria outlined in Article 68(2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: For the purposes of paragraph 1, the Commission may request access to the general-purpose AI model concerned through APIs or further appropriate technical means and tools, including source code.
texts.text: For the purposes of paragraph 1, the Commission may request access to the general-purpose AI model concerned through APIs or further appropriate technical means and tools, including source code.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The request for access shall state the legal basis, the purpose and reasons of the request and set the period within which the access is to be provided, and the fines provided for in Article 101 for failure to provide access.
texts.text: The request for access shall state the legal basis, the purpose and reasons of the request and set the period within which the access is to be provided, and the fines provided for in Article 101 for failure to provide access.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The providers of the general-purpose AI model concerned or its representative shall supply the information requested. In the case of legal persons, companies or firms, or where the provider has no legal personality, the persons authorised to represent them by law or by their statutes, shall provide the access requested on behalf of the provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned.
texts.text: The providers of the general-purpose AI model concerned or its representative shall supply the information requested. In the case of legal persons, companies or firms, or where the provider has no legal personality, the persons authorised to represent them by law or by their statutes, shall provide the access requested on behalf of the provider of the general-purpose AI model concerned.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall adopt implementing acts setting out the detailed arrangements and the conditions for the evaluations, including the detailed arrangements for involving independent experts, and the procedure for the selection thereof. Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 98(2).
texts.text: The Commission shall adopt implementing acts setting out the detailed arrangements and the conditions for the evaluations, including the detailed arrangements for involving independent experts, and the procedure for the selection thereof. Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 98(2).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Prior to requesting access to the general-purpose AI model concerned, the AI Office may initiate a structured dialogue with the provider of the general-purpose AI model to gather more information on the internal testing of the model, internal safeguards for preventing systemic risks, and other internal procedures and measures the provider has taken to mitigate such risks.
texts.text: Prior to requesting access to the general-purpose AI model concerned, the AI Office may initiate a structured dialogue with the provider of the general-purpose AI model to gather more information on the internal testing of the model, internal safeguards for preventing systemic risks, and other internal procedures and measures the provider has taken to mitigate such risks.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  93
texts.text: Article  93

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Power  to request  measures
texts.text: Power  to request  measures

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Where necessary and appropriate, the Commission may request providers to:
texts.text: Where necessary and appropriate, the Commission may request providers to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) take appropriate measures to comply with the obligations set out in Articles 53 and 54;
texts.text: (a) take appropriate measures to comply with the obligations set out in Articles 53 and 54;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) implement mitigation measures, where the evaluation carried out in accordance with Article 92 has given rise to serious and substantiated concern of a systemic risk at Union level;
texts.text: (b) implement mitigation measures, where the evaluation carried out in accordance with Article 92 has given rise to serious and substantiated concern of a systemic risk at Union level;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) restrict the making available on the market, withdraw or recall the model.
texts.text: (c) restrict the making available on the market, withdraw or recall the model.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Before a measure is requested, the AI Office may initiate a structured dialogue with the provider of the general-purpose AI model.
texts.text: Before a measure is requested, the AI Office may initiate a structured dialogue with the provider of the general-purpose AI model.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If, during the structured dialogue referred to in paragraph 2, the provider of the general-purpose AI model with systemic risk offers commitments to implement mitigation measures to address a systemic risk at Union level, the Commission may, by decision, make those commitments binding and declare that there are no further grounds for action.
texts.text: If, during the structured dialogue referred to in paragraph 2, the provider of the general-purpose AI model with systemic risk offers commitments to implement mitigation measures to address a systemic risk at Union level, the Commission may, by decision, make those commitments binding and declare that there are no further grounds for action.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  94
texts.text: Article  94

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Procedural rights of economic operators of  the general-purpose AI model
texts.text: Procedural rights of economic operators of  the general-purpose AI model

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Article  18  of  Regulation  (EU)  2019/1020  shall  apply mutatis  mutandis to  the  providers  of  the  general-purpose  AI  model, without prejudice  to more  specific  procedural  rights  provided  for  in  this  Regulation.
texts.text: Article  18  of  Regulation  (EU)  2019/1020  shall  apply mutatis  mutandis to  the  providers  of  the  general-purpose  AI  model, without prejudice  to more  specific  procedural  rights  provided  for  in  this  Regulation.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: CHAPTER X
texts.text: CHAPTER X

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: CODES OF CONDUCT AND GUIDELINES
texts.text: CODES OF CONDUCT AND GUIDELINES

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  95
texts.text: Article  95

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Codes of conduct for  voluntary application of specific requirements
texts.text: Codes of conduct for  voluntary application of specific requirements

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The AI Office and the Member States shall encourage and facilitate the drawing up of codes of conduct, including related governance mechanisms, intended to foster the voluntary application to AI systems, other than high-risk AI systems, of some or all of the requirements set out in Chapter III, Section 2 taking into account the available technical solutions and industry best practices allowing for the application of such requirements.
texts.text: The AI Office and the Member States shall encourage and facilitate the drawing up of codes of conduct, including related governance mechanisms, intended to foster the voluntary application to AI systems, other than high-risk AI systems, of some or all of the requirements set out in Chapter III, Section 2 taking into account the available technical solutions and industry best practices allowing for the application of such requirements.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The AI Office and the Member States shall facilitate the drawing up of codes of conduct concerning the voluntary application, including by deployers, of specific requirements to all AI systems, on the basis of clear objectives and key performance indicators to measure the achievement of those objectives, including elements such as, but not limited to:
texts.text: The AI Office and the Member States shall facilitate the drawing up of codes of conduct concerning the voluntary application, including by deployers, of specific requirements to all AI systems, on the basis of clear objectives and key performance indicators to measure the achievement of those objectives, including elements such as, but not limited to:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) applicable elements provided for in Union ethical guidelines for trustworthy AI;
texts.text: (a) applicable elements provided for in Union ethical guidelines for trustworthy AI;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) assessing and minimising the impact of AI systems on environmental sustainability, including as regards energy-efficient programming and techniques for the efficient design, training and use of AI;
texts.text: (b) assessing and minimising the impact of AI systems on environmental sustainability, including as regards energy-efficient programming and techniques for the efficient design, training and use of AI;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) promoting AI literacy, in particular that of persons dealing with the development, operation and use of AI;
texts.text: (c) promoting AI literacy, in particular that of persons dealing with the development, operation and use of AI;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) facilitating an inclusive and diverse design of AI systems, including through the establishment of inclusive and diverse development teams and the promotion of stakeholders' participation in that process;
texts.text: (d) facilitating an inclusive and diverse design of AI systems, including through the establishment of inclusive and diverse development teams and the promotion of stakeholders' participation in that process;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) assessing and preventing the negative impact of AI systems on vulnerable persons or groups of vulnerable persons, including as regards accessibility for persons with a disability, as well as on gender equality.
texts.text: (e) assessing and preventing the negative impact of AI systems on vulnerable persons or groups of vulnerable persons, including as regards accessibility for persons with a disability, as well as on gender equality.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Codes of conduct may be drawn up by individual providers or deployers of AI systems or by organisations representing them or by both, including with the involvement of any interested stakeholders and their representative organisations, including civil society organisations and academia. Codes of conduct may cover one or more AI systems taking into account the similarity of the intended purpose of the relevant systems.
texts.text: Codes of conduct may be drawn up by individual providers or deployers of AI systems or by organisations representing them or by both, including with the involvement of any interested stakeholders and their representative organisations, including civil society organisations and academia. Codes of conduct may cover one or more AI systems taking into account the similarity of the intended purpose of the relevant systems.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The AI Office and the Member States shall take into account the specific interests and needs of SMEs, including start-ups, when encouraging and facilitating the drawing up of codes of conduct.
texts.text: The AI Office and the Member States shall take into account the specific interests and needs of SMEs, including start-ups, when encouraging and facilitating the drawing up of codes of conduct.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  96
texts.text: Article  96

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Guidelines from the Commission on the implementation of  this Regulation
texts.text: Guidelines from the Commission on the implementation of  this Regulation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The Commission shall develop guidelines on the practical implementation of this Regulation, and in particular on:
texts.text: The Commission shall develop guidelines on the practical implementation of this Regulation, and in particular on:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (a) the application of the requirements and obligations referred to in Articles 8 to 15 and in Article 25;
texts.text: (a) the application of the requirements and obligations referred to in Articles 8 to 15 and in Article 25;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (b) the prohibited practices referred to in Article 5;
texts.text: (b) the prohibited practices referred to in Article 5;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (c) the practical implementation of the provisions related to substantial modification;
texts.text: (c) the practical implementation of the provisions related to substantial modification;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (d) the practical implementation of transparency obligations laid down in Article 50;
texts.text: (d) the practical implementation of transparency obligations laid down in Article 50;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (e) detailed information on the relationship of this Regulation with the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I, as well as with other relevant Union law, including as regards consistency in their enforcement;
texts.text: (e) detailed information on the relationship of this Regulation with the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I, as well as with other relevant Union law, including as regards consistency in their enforcement;

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (f) the application of the definition of an AI system as set out in Article 3, point (1).
texts.text: (f) the application of the definition of an AI system as set out in Article 3, point (1).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When issuing such guidelines, the Commission shall pay particular attention to the needs of SMEs including start-ups, of local  public  authorities  and  of  the  sectors  most  likely  to  be  affected  by  this  Regulation.
texts.text: When issuing such guidelines, the Commission shall pay particular attention to the needs of SMEs including start-ups, of local  public  authorities  and  of  the  sectors  most  likely  to  be  affected  by  this  Regulation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The guidelines referred to in the first subparagraph of this paragraph shall take due account of the generally acknowledged state  of  the  art  on  AI,  as  well  as  of  relevant  harmonised  standards  and  common  specifications  that  are  referred  to  in Articles  40  and  41,  or  of  those  harmonised  standards  or  technical  specifications  that  are  set  out  pursuant  to  Union harmonisation law.
texts.text: The guidelines referred to in the first subparagraph of this paragraph shall take due account of the generally acknowledged state  of  the  art  on  AI,  as  well  as  of  relevant  harmonised  standards  and  common  specifications  that  are  referred  to  in Articles  40  and  41,  or  of  those  harmonised  standards  or  technical  specifications  that  are  set  out  pursuant  to  Union harmonisation law.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: At the request of the Member States or the AI Office, or on its own initiative, the Commission shall update guidelines previously adopted when deemed necessary.
texts.text: At the request of the Member States or the AI Office, or on its own initiative, the Commission shall update guidelines previously adopted when deemed necessary.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: CHAPTER XI
texts.text: CHAPTER XI

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: DELEGATION OF POWER AND COMMITTEE PROCEDURE
texts.text: DELEGATION OF POWER AND COMMITTEE PROCEDURE

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Article  97
texts.text: Article  97

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Exercise  of  the  delegation
texts.text: Exercise  of  the  delegation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The power to adopt delegated acts is conferred on the Commission subject to the conditions laid down in this Article.
texts.text: The power to adopt delegated acts is conferred on the Commission subject to the conditions laid down in this Article.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The power to adopt delegated acts referred to in Article 6(6) and (7), Article 7(1) and (3), Article 11(3), Article 43(5) and (6), Article 47(5), Article 51(3), Article 52(4) and Article 53(5) and (6) shall be conferred on the Commission for a period of five years from 1 August 2024. The Commission shall draw up a report in respect of the delegation of power not later than nine months before the end of the five-year period. The delegation of power shall be tacitly extended for periods of an identical duration, unless the European Parliament or the Council opposes such extension not later than three months before the end of each period.
texts.text: The power to adopt delegated acts referred to in Article 6(6) and (7), Article 7(1) and (3), Article 11(3), Article 43(5) and (6), Article 47(5), Article 51(3), Article 52(4) and Article 53(5) and (6) shall be conferred on the Commission for a period of five years from 1 August 2024. The Commission shall draw up a report in respect of the delegation of power not later than nine months before the end of the five-year period. The delegation of power shall be tacitly extended for periods of an identical duration, unless the European Parliament or the Council opposes such extension not later than three months before the end of each period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The delegation of power referred to in Article 6(6) and (7), Article 7(1) and (3), Article 11(3), Article 43(5) and (6), Article 47(5), Article 51(3), Article 52(4) and Article 53(5) and (6) may be revoked at any time by the European Parliament or by the Council. A decision of revocation shall put an end to the delegation of power specified in that decision. It shall take effect the day following that of its publication in the Official Journal of the European Union or at a later date specified therein. It shall not affect the validity of any delegated acts already in force.
texts.text: The delegation of power referred to in Article 6(6) and (7), Article 7(1) and (3), Article 11(3), Article 43(5) and (6), Article 47(5), Article 51(3), Article 52(4) and Article 53(5) and (6) may be revoked at any time by the European Parliament or by the Council. A decision of revocation shall put an end to the delegation of power specified in that decision. It shall take effect the day following that of its publication in the Official Journal of the European Union or at a later date specified therein. It shall not affect the validity of any delegated acts already in force.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Before adopting a delegated act, the Commission shall consult experts designated by each Member State in accordance with the principles laid down in the Interinstitutional Agreement of 13 April 2016 on Better Law-Making.
texts.text: Before adopting a delegated act, the Commission shall consult experts designated by each Member State in accordance with the principles laid down in the Interinstitutional Agreement of 13 April 2016 on Better Law-Making.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: As soon as it adopts a delegated act, the Commission shall notify it simultaneously to the European Parliament and to the Council.
texts.text: As soon as it adopts a delegated act, the Commission shall notify it simultaneously to the European Parliament and to the Council.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Any delegated act adopted pursuant to Article 6(6) or (7), Article 7(1) or (3), Article 11(3), Article 43(5) or (6), Article 47(5), Article 51(3), Article 52(4) or Article 53(5) or (6) shall enter into force only if no objection has been expressed by either the European Parliament or the Council within a period of three months of notification of that act to the European Parliament and the Council or if, before the expiry of that period, the European Parliament and the Council have both informed the Commission that they will not object. That period shall be extended by three months at the initiative of the European Parliament or of the Council.
texts.text: Any delegated act adopted pursuant to Article 6(6) or (7), Article 7(1) or (3), Article 11(3), Article 43(5) or (6), Article 47(5), Article 51(3), Article 52(4) or Article 53(5) or (6) shall enter into force only if no objection has been expressed by either the European Parliament or the Council within a period of three months of notification of that act to the European Parliament and the Council or if, before the expiry of that period, the European Parliament and the Council have both informed the Commission that they will not object. That period shall be extended by three months at the initiative of the European Parliament or of the Council.

texts.text: The Commission shall be assisted by a committee. That committee shall be a committee within the meaning of Regulation (EU) No 182/2011.
